channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Microsoft claims Edge had a great year, boasts soaring browser user numbers
item.description: Microsoft claims 10 billion GenAI chats happened from within Edge, but browser still lags behind Chrome.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: AI, power availability and Intel’s future
item.description: As 2025 kicks off, it’s time to dust off the crystal ball. Here are my data center predictions for the coming year. 1) Trump backs off on tech tariffs For all the handwringing, Trump has a history of not following through on his threats.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Musk defends demonetizing critics' content on X
item.description: Tech billionaire Elon Musk defended his move to demonetize his critics' content on his social platform X and maintained the decision did not violate free speech. "The first amendment is protection for 'free speech', not 'paid speech' ffs," Musk wrote Wednesday on X.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: Trump blames ‘open borders’ for New Orleans, Las Vegas attacks
item.description: President-elect Trump blamed the situation at the southern border for the recent attacks in New Orleans and Las Vegas, railing against President Biden for his immigration policies as both cases are being investigated.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, The Hill

item.title: Apple could launch the iPhone SE 4 as the iPhone 16E, according to rumors
item.description: The handset previously known as the iPhone SE 4 could in fact become the iPhone 16E, according to leaks.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: 4 keys for writing cross-platform apps
item.description: These days, most every application has the potential to be a cross-platform application. That’s great for users but often difficult for developers.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: How to use Gemini AI to make templates in Google Sheets
item.description: Need ideas or motivation to help you build a spreadsheet in Google Sheets? You can browse through the templates that are included in this office app and select one to customize. But a more intriguing option is to use the tool in Sheets called Help Me Organize.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Honey browser extension accused of scamming influencers, users
item.description: In the the 23-minute video by user MegaLag, the PayPal property is accused of abusing the affiliate link system for its own profit.
item.dc:creator: Anna Kutz, NewsNation

item.title: Ford’s X account calls Israel a ‘terrorist state’ in apparent hack incident
item.description: The official account for Ford Motor Co. on the social platform X was apparently compromised in a hacking incident behind a viral post calling Israel a “terrorist state.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, The Hill

item.title: Venezuela's top court issues a $10 million fine for TikTok over allegedly deadly video challenges
item.description: Venezuela’s Supreme Court has issued a $10 million fine against TikTok for “not implementing measures” to prevent viral video challenges that have allegedly led to the deaths of three Venezuelan children recently.
item.dc:creator: Juan Pablo Arraez And Manuel Rueda, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Rhode Islanders' data was leaked from a cyberattack on state health benefits website
item.description: PROVIDENCE, R.I. (AP) — Cybercriminals who hacked Rhode Island’s system for health and benefits programs have released files to a site on the dark web, a scenario the state has been preparing for, Gov. Daniel McKee said Monday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Small businesses brace themselves for potentially disruptive TikTok ban
item.description: A looming TikTok ban could affect the millions of small businesses that use the short-video social media app to grow their business.
item.dc:creator: Mae Anderson, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: In 2024, artificial intelligence was all about putting AI tools to work
item.description: Just over two years after the debut of ChatGPT, the world is getting a little savvier about what artificial intelligence technology can do for us and what it’s not so great at.
item.dc:creator: Matt O'Brien And Sarah Parvini, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: The future of open source will be messy
item.description: Open Source Initiative (OSI) chief Stefano Maffulli says Meta is “bullying” the industry on the concept of open source. The Economist more colorfully characterizes the dispute as “bare-it-all purists … confronting Meta, the social-media giant controlled by a mankini-clad Mark Zuckerberg.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Got an Apple computer for Christmas? Here are 6 apps and games to try with that new Mac
item.description: So you woke up on Christmas morning to a new Mac. Perhaps it’s the miraculous M4 Mac mini, or maybe it’s the stunning M4 Pro MacBook Pro complete with the nano texture display.
item.dc:creator: Michael Grothaus, Fast Company

item.title: New York state government to monitor its use of AI under a new law
item.description: ALBANY, N.Y. (AP) — New York state government agencies will have to conduct reviews and publish reports that detail how they're using artificial intelligence software, under a new law signed by Gov. Kathy Hochul.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Lincoln returns to port after proving out ‘game changing’ connectivity
item.description: On its latest deployment, the Nimitz-class carrier went from slow military satellite uplinks to modern capabilities that move terabytes of data each day. The post Lincoln returns to port after proving out ‘game changing’ connectivity first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Jared Serbu, Federal News Network

item.title: Mexico tests cellphone app allowing migrants to send alert if they are about to be detained in US
item.description: MEXICO CITY (AP) — Mexico is developing a cellphone app that will allow migrants to warn relatives and local consulates if they think they are about to be detained by the U.S. immigration department, a senior official said Friday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KREX 5
